reviewSummary.summary: Core login controls are present, but there are duplicate password elements, weak naming, and brittle locator strategies. The artifact can be improved by removing duplicates, standardizing semantic naming, and strengthening locators to better support downstream automation.

issues.message: Two elements represent the same password input field with different locators.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep a single password_textbox element using the stronger locator id=password and remove the placeholder-based duplicate.

issues.message: Element name 'password' is ambiguous and does not follow semantic naming used elsewhere.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename to password_textbox to clearly represent the control type and maintain naming consistency with username_textbox.

issues.message: The name contains an unnecessary underscore separation and is slightly inconsistent with common login naming conventions.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename to username_textbox for cleaner and more conventional semantic naming.

issues.message: Locator uses placeholder text which can change with localization or UI updates.
issues.suggested_fix: Prefer the stable attribute locator xpath=//input[@name='ion-input-0'] or a unique form control attribute if available.

issues.message: Locator contains fallback logic using normalize-space text which may be unreliable since the button uses an icon.
issues.suggested_fix: Use a locator based on the icon aria-label such as xpath=//ion-fab-button[.//ion-icon[@aria-label='home']].

issues.message: Locator relies partly on visible text even though the control is icon-based.
issues.suggested_fix: Use xpath=//ion-fab-button[.//ion-icon[@aria-label='arrow back']] to rely on the icon accessibility label.

issues.message: Password field is classified as textbox even though it is a password input.
issues.suggested_fix: Classify the element type as password for clearer semantic modeling.

issues.message: No element representing authentication error or validation message is captured, even though the workflow includes failed login scenarios.
issues.suggested_fix: If present in the UI, capture a login_error_message or authentication_error_message element to support stronger validation of failed login scenarios.

approved_elements.name: username_textbox
approved_elements.description: Field used to enter the user's username for authentication.

approved_elements.name: password_textbox
approved_elements.description: Field used to enter the user's password for authentication.

approved_elements.name: sign_in_button
approved_elements.description: Primary button used to submit the login form.

approved_elements.name: home_navigation_button
approved_elements.description: Floating navigation button that navigates to the home page.

approved_elements.name: back_navigation_button
approved_elements.description: Floating navigation button used to navigate back to the previous or home page.
